_schema: 6-beat structure per CLAUDE.md law 5. Prose drafted from placeholders.
_status: ai_draft_needs_director_curation (2026-07-06) — all prose is AI draft

intro_cold.speaker: dee
intro_cold.text:
Feed's on the shelf.

Register's up front.
variants:
If it's urgent, say so.

Otherwise I'm elbow-deep in inventory.

options.text: (Buy what you came for and go)

intro.speaker: dee
intro.text:
Boots say chickens.

Am I close?
variants: Let me guess — something's off its feed and you waited three days to come in.

Grab what you need.

Holler if a label doesn't make sense; half of them don't.

Afternoon.

If you're here about layer feed, buy the plain one. The fancy bag is the same feed with a sunset on it.

text_rules.text:
There's coffee on in the back if you want it.

Still owe you for that weekend you helped me worm the Kessler herd — that was a two-person job I'd been pretending was a one-person job.

options.text: "Would you look over my flock when you get a minute?"
"Can you carry the feed bill till my check clears?"

intro_trusted.speaker: dee
intro_trusted.text:
Good, you're here.

I set aside a bag of the good layer feed before the price went up — it's under the counter with your name on it, literally, in marker.

How're the birds?

And don't say fine, tell me actual things.
variants:
Sit a minute.

I've been on four farms since sunup and you're the first person today whose animals I'm not worried about.

That's a compliment, take it.

Coffee's fresh-ish.

checkup.speaker: dee
checkup.text:
Birds look decent.

One hen's carrying a wing low — keep her out of the mud for a week and she'll sort herself.

Cheaper than calling me later, and I say that as the person you'd be calling.

bill_carried.speaker: dee
bill_carried.text:
Till the fifteenth.

I've carried worse for people I liked less.

bill_refused.speaker: dee
bill_refused.text:
Can't this month.

Feed man doesn't carry me, so I can't carry you.

It ain't personal.

chat.speaker: dee
chat.text:
Scours went through two dairies north of town.

Contained now, but wash your boots if you've been up that way.
variants:
Foot rot season.

Everybody's pasture is soup and everybody's surprised, same as last year.

Kesslers lost a calf Tuesday.

Nothing anybody did wrong. Some springs are just like that.

Somebody's dog has been running stock out past the creek.

If it's yours, this is me telling you nicely, once.

Fly counts are up early.

Get your tags on before it costs you weight.
